proud.en: feeling pleased and satisfied about something you or someone close to you has done

ex.en: She felt proud of her son's achievement.

prove.en: show that something is true by using evidence or argument

ex.en: She proved her theory with new data.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새로운 자료로 자신의 이론을 증명했다.

prove.en: be found or shown to be a particular thing over time

ex.en: The plan proved to be a success.

proverb.en: a short well-known saying that expresses a general truth or piece of advice

ex.en: 'Practice makes perfect' is a common proverb.

provided.en: used to say that something will happen only if something else happens first

ex.en: You can go out, provided you finish your homework.

psychological.en: relating to the mind or to the way people think and feel

ex.en: The stress had a serious psychological effect on her.
ex.ko: 그 스트레스는 그녀에게 심각한 심리적 영향을 미쳤다.

pub.en: a place, especially in Britain, where people go to drink and meet friends

ex.en: We had a drink at the local pub.

public transport.en: buses, trains, and other vehicles that anyone can use to travel

ex.en: She takes public transport to work every day.

publicly.en: in a way that is open for everyone to see or know about

ex.en: He publicly apologized for the mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 그 실수에 대해 공개적으로 사과했다.

publisher.en: a company or person that prepares and sells books, magazines, or music

ex.en: The publisher released her new novel this month.
ex.ko: 그 출판사는 이번 달에 그녀의 새 소설을 출간했다.

pullover.en: a sweater that you put on by pulling it over your head

ex.en: He wore a warm pullover in winter.

pump.en: a machine that pushes liquid or gas from one place to another

ex.en: They used a pump to get water out of the basement.
ex.ko: 그들은 지하실의 물을 빼내기 위해 펌프를 사용했다.

pump.en: move liquid or gas somewhere using a pump

ex.en: Workers pumped oil through the pipeline.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 송유관을 통해 석유를 퍼 올렸다.

punish.en: make someone suffer because they have done something wrong

ex.en: The teacher punished the students for cheating.

punishment.en: a penalty given to someone for doing something wrong

ex.en: He received a harsh punishment for the crime.
ex.ko: 그는 그 범죄에 대해 가혹한 처벌을 받았다.

pupil.en: a child who is being taught, especially in school

ex.en: The teacher praised her pupil for the essay.

pupil.en: the small black circular opening in the center of the eye

ex.en: The doctor checked the size of his pupil.
They adopted a puppy from the shelter.
The bottle contains pure orange juice.
ex.ko: 그 병에는 순수한 오렌지 주스가 들어 있다.

pure.en: complete or total, used to emphasize something

ex.en: It was pure luck that we won.

purify.en: remove dirty or harmful substances from something to make it clean

ex.en: The machine purifies the water before it reaches the tap.
ex.ko: 그 기계는 물이 수도꼭지에 닿기 전에 정화한다.

purse.en: a small bag used for carrying money and personal items, especially by women

ex.en: She kept her keys in her purse.

puzzle.en: make someone feel confused because they cannot understand something

ex.en: Her strange behavior puzzled everyone.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이상한 행동은 모두를 당혹하게 했다.

puzzle.en: a game or problem that tests your thinking skills

ex.en: He solved the puzzle in ten minutes.

quake.en: a sudden shaking of the ground, short for earthquake

ex.en: The quake shook buildings across the city.
ex.ko: 그 지진은 도시 전역의 건물을 흔들었다.

qualified.en: having the necessary skills, knowledge, or experience for a job or task

ex.en: She is a qualified doctor.

qualified.en: limited or restricted in some way, not complete

ex.en: He gave the plan only qualified support.

qualify.en: reach the standard or meet the conditions needed to do or get something

ex.en: She qualified for the final round.

quantity.en: an amount of something that can be counted or measured

ex.en: They bought a large quantity of rice.

question mark.en: the punctuation mark (?) used at the end of a question

ex.en: Don't forget the question mark at the end.
ex.ko: 끝에 물음표 넣는 거 잊지 마.

questionnaire.en: a written set of questions used to collect information from people

ex.en: Please fill out this questionnaire.

queue.en: stand in a line waiting for something

ex.en: We queued for an hour to get tickets.
ex.ko: 우리는 표를 사려고 한 시간 동안 줄을 섰다.

queue.en: a line of people or vehicles waiting for something

ex.en: There was a long queue outside the store.

race.en: a competition to see who is the fastest

ex.en: He won the race easily.

race.en: a group of people who share the same ancestry or physical characteristics

ex.en: People of every race attended the festival.
ex.ko: 모든 인종의 사람들이 그 축제에 참여했다.

race.en: move or compete at high speed

ex.en: The kids raced each other to the gate.
The company has a strict policy against racial discrimination.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 인종 차별에 반대하는 엄격한 정책을 두고 있다.

radiation.en: energy that is sent out in the form of waves or particles, sometimes harmful

ex.en: The workers wore suits to protect them from radiation.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 방사선으로부터 보호하기 위해 보호복을 입었다.

rage.en: a strong feeling of extreme anger

ex.en: He shouted in a fit of rage.

rail.en: a bar fixed along or around something for people to hold onto

ex.en: Hold the rail as you go down the stairs.

rail.en: a metal bar that forms the track for a train

ex.en: The train runs smoothly along the rails.

railroad.en: a system of tracks that trains travel on, especially in American English

ex.en: The railroad connects the two cities.

railway.en: a system of tracks that trains travel on, especially in British English

ex.en: They traveled by railway across Europe.

rainfall.en: the amount of rain that falls in a particular place and time

ex.en: This region gets heavy rainfall in summer.

rainforest.en: a thick forest found in hot, wet parts of the world

ex.en: The Amazon rainforest is home to many species.
ex.ko: 아마존 열대우림에는 많은 종이 살고 있다.

rank.en: a position or level in an organization, such as the army

ex.en: He rose to the rank of captain.

rank.en: give something a position on a scale compared with others

ex.en: The team ranks first in the league.

rapid.en: happening quickly or in a short time

ex.en: The company has seen rapid growth this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 급속한 성장을 보였다.

ex.en: Prices rose rapidly last month.
It's rare to see snow here.
ex.ko: 여기서 눈을 보는 건 드문 일이다.

rare.en: (of meat) cooked for only a short time so it is still red inside

ex.en: He ordered his steak rare.
She rarely eats fast food.

raspberry.en: a small soft red fruit that grows on a bush

ex.en: She made jam with fresh raspberries.

ration.en: a fixed amount of food or goods given to each person, especially when supplies are limited

ex.en: Soldiers received a daily ration of food.
ex.ko: 군인들은 매일 정해진 배급 식량을 받았다.

rational.en: based on clear thinking and reason rather than emotion

ex.en: We need to make a rational decision.

razor.en: a tool with a sharp blade used for shaving hair off the skin

ex.en: He shaved with a razor every morning.

react.en: behave or respond in a particular way because of something that happens

ex.en: She reacted calmly to the bad news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 나쁜 소식에 침착하게 반응했다.

realistic.en: seeing things as they really are and being sensible about what can be achieved

ex.en: We need a realistic plan for the budget.
ex.ko: 우리는 예산에 대한 현실적인 계획이 필요하다.

reality.en: the way things actually are, not how they are imagined

ex.en: The dream finally became reality.

rearrange.en: change the order or position of things

ex.en: She rearranged the furniture in her room.
That sounds like a reasonable explanation.

reasonable.en: (of a price) fair and not too expensive

ex.en: The hotel offers rooms at a reasonable price.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 적당한 가격에 방을 제공한다.

rebuild.en: build something again after it has been damaged or destroyed

ex.en: They rebuilt the house after the fire.
ex.ko: 그들은 화재 이후 그 집을 재건했다.

recall.en: an official request to return a faulty product or to bring back military forces

ex.en: The car company issued a recall of the model.
ex.ko: 그 자동차 회사는 그 모델에 대한 회수를 발표했다.

ex.en: I can't recall her name right now.

reception.en: the way people respond to something, especially the way they welcome it

ex.en: The new policy got a mixed reception.
ex.ko: 그 새 정책은 엇갈린 반응을 받았다.

reception.en: the place in a hotel or office where visitors are greeted

ex.en: Please leave your bag at reception.

recite.en: say a poem or piece of writing aloud from memory

ex.en: The student recited the poem perfectly.

reclaim.en: get something back that was taken away or lost

ex.en: She reclaimed her luggage at the airport.

recognize.en: know someone or something because you have seen or experienced them before

ex.en: I almost didn't recognize you with short hair.
ex.ko: 짧은 머리라서 하마터면 못 알아볼 뻔했어.

recognize.en: officially accept that something is true or valid

ex.en: The company recognized her hard work.

recommend.en: suggest that someone should do something or use something because it is good

ex.en: My friend recommended this restaurant.

recording.en: a piece of music, sound, or video that has been recorded

ex.en: They listened to a recording of the interview.

recover.en: become well again after being ill or hurt

ex.en: It took weeks for him to recover from the flu.
ex.ko: 그가 독감에서 회복하는 데 몇 주가 걸렸다.

recover.en: get back something that was lost or taken away

ex.en: Police recovered the stolen car.

recovery.en: the process of becoming well or normal again after being sick or damaged

ex.en: She made a full recovery after the surgery.

recycled.en: made from materials that have been used before and processed for reuse

ex.en: This notebook is made of recycled paper.

recycling.en: the process of turning waste into something that can be used again

ex.en: Our town has a good recycling program.
ex.ko: 우리 동네는 재활용 프로그램이 잘 되어 있다.

reduce.en: make something smaller in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: The company reduced its prices by 20 percent.

reduction.en: the act of making something smaller or less in amount

ex.en: There was a big reduction in traffic accidents.

reef.en: a long line of rocks or coral lying just below the surface of the sea

ex.en: Divers explored the coral reef.

referee.en: the official who makes sure players follow the rules in a sports game

ex.en: The referee blew the whistle for a foul.

reflection.en: an image of something seen in a mirror, glass, or water

ex.en: She looked at her reflection in the mirror.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거울에 비친 자신의 모습을 봤다.

ex.en: After some reflection, he changed his mind.
ex.ko: 곰곰이 생각한 끝에 그는 마음을 바꿨다.

refreshments.en: small amounts of food and drink offered at an event

ex.en: Refreshments will be served after the meeting.

refund.en: an amount of money given back to you, for example because you returned a product

ex.en: She asked for a refund on the broken item.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고장난 물건에 대해 환불을 요청했다.

refusal.en: an act of saying no to something offered or requested

ex.en: His refusal to apologize made things worse.
ex.ko: 그가 사과를 거절해서 상황이 더 나빠졌다.

refuse.en: say that you will not do, accept, or allow something

ex.en: He refused to answer the question.

regain.en: get something back that you had before, such as strength or control

ex.en: She slowly regained her strength after the illness.
ex.ko: 그녀는 병을 앓고 나서 서서히 체력을 되찾았다.

regard.en: a particular aspect or detail of something

ex.en: In this regard, the new policy is a big improvement.
ex.ko: 이 점에서, 새 정책은 큰 개선이다.

regard.en: think of someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: She is regarded as one of the best writers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 최고의 작가 중 한 명으로 여겨진다.

regarding.en: about or concerning a particular subject

ex.en: I have a question regarding the schedule.

region.en: a large area of a country or the world

ex.en: This region is known for its wine.
The festival features regional food and music.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 지역 음식과 음악을 선보인다.

register.en: put your name on an official list for a course, event, or service

ex.en: Students must register for classes online.

register.en: an official list or book of names or information

ex.en: The teacher checked the class register.

registration.en: the act of putting your name on an official list

ex.en: Registration for the course closes on Friday.

regulation.en: an official rule that controls how something is done

ex.en: The factory must follow safety regulations.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 안전 규정을 따라야 한다.

reject.en: refuse to accept, believe, or agree with something

ex.en: The bank rejected his loan application.
This chart relates income to education level.
ex.ko: 이 도표는 소득을 교육 수준과 연관 짓는다.

relate.en: understand and feel sympathy for someone's feelings or situation

ex.en: I really relate to what she went through.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 겪은 일에 정말 공감이 된다.

relation.en: the way in which two or more things are connected

ex.en: There is a close relation between diet and health.
ex.ko: 식습관과 건강 사이에는 밀접한 관계가 있다.

relationship.en: the way two or more people or groups feel about and behave towards each other

ex.en: They have a strong relationship built on trust.
ex.ko: 그들은 신뢰를 바탕으로 한 끈끈한 관계를 맺고 있다.

ex.en: We invited all our relatives to the wedding.

relative.en: considered in comparison with something else

ex.en: The two cities are similar in relative size.

relatively.en: to a fairly noticeable degree, especially in comparison to something else

ex.en: The exam was relatively easy this year.

relaxing.en: making you feel calm and free from stress

ex.en: We had a relaxing weekend at the beach.

reliable.en: able to be trusted to do what is expected

ex.en: He is a reliable friend who never lets me down.
ex.ko: 그는 나를 실망시킨 적 없는 믿을 수 있는 친구다.

religion.en: a belief in a god or gods, or a system of belief and worship

ex.en: They studied different religions at school.

religious.en: relating to religion or belief in a god

ex.en: She comes from a religious family.

rely.en: depend on someone or something for help or support

ex.en: You can rely on him to finish the job.
ex.ko: 그가 그 일을 끝내리라고 믿어도 된다.

remainder.en: the part of something that is left after the rest has gone

ex.en: She spent the remainder of the day reading.

remark.en: a short comment or opinion about something

ex.en: He made a rude remark about her cooking.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 요리에 대해 무례한 발언을 했다.

ex.en: She remarked that the movie was too long.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 영화가 너무 길다고 언급했다.

remarkable.en: unusual or impressive enough to be noticed

ex.en: She made remarkable progress in just a month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 단 한 달 만에 놀라운 발전을 이루었다.

remedy.en: something that cures an illness or solves a problem

ex.en: Honey and tea is a common remedy for a sore throat.
ex.ko: 꿀차는 목 아플 때 흔히 쓰는 치료법이다.

remembrance.en: the act of remembering and honoring someone or something, especially the dead

ex.en: They held a moment of silence in remembrance of the victims.
ex.ko: 그들은 희생자들을 추모하며 묵념의 시간을 가졌다.

remote control.en: a device used to operate a machine, such as a TV, from a distance

ex.en: Where did I put the remote control?

remove.en: take something away from a place or get rid of it

ex.en: She removed the stain from the shirt.

renew.en: make an agreement or license valid for a further period of time

ex.en: I need to renew my passport next year.

repay.en: pay back money that you owe someone

ex.en: He repaid the loan within a year.

repeatedly.en: many times, over and over again

ex.en: She repeatedly asked him the same question.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 같은 질문을 반복해서 물었다.

representative.en: a person chosen to speak or act on behalf of others

ex.en: A representative from the company attended the meeting.

reproduce.en: make a copy of something or produce it again

ex.en: The artist reproduced the painting in smaller size.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 그 그림을 더 작은 크기로 복제했다.

reproduce.en: produce offspring, as animals or plants do

ex.en: Rabbits reproduce very quickly.

republic.en: a country governed by elected representatives rather than a king or queen

ex.en: France became a republic after the revolution.

reputation.en: the general opinion that people have about someone or something

ex.en: The restaurant has a good reputation for its food.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 음식으로 좋은 평판을 가지고 있다.

require.en: need something or make something necessary

ex.en: The job requires good communication skills.
ex.ko: 그 일은 좋은 의사소통 능력을 필요로 한다.

requirement.en: something that is needed or demanded

ex.en: A university degree is a requirement for this job.

rescue.en: save someone or something from a dangerous situation

ex.en: Firefighters rescued the cat from the tree.

rescue.en: an act of saving someone from danger

ex.en: The rescue took three hours.
ex.ko: 그 구조 작업은 세 시간이 걸렸다.

researcher.en: a person who studies a subject in order to discover new information

ex.en: Researchers are studying the effects of the new drug.
ex.ko: 연구원들은 그 신약의 효과를 연구하고 있다.

resemble.en: look or be like someone or something else

ex.en: She resembles her mother a lot.

reservation.en: an arrangement to have a room, table, or seat kept for you

ex.en: I made a reservation at the restaurant for two.
ex.ko: 나는 그 식당에 두 명 예약을 했다.

reservation.en: a feeling of doubt about a plan or idea

ex.en: He had some reservations about the new plan.
ex.ko: 그는 그 새 계획에 대해 다소 의구심이 있었다.

reserve.en: arrange to have something kept for you in advance

ex.en: We reserved a table for dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁 식사를 위해 테이블을 예약했다.

reserve.en: keep something for later use instead of using it now

ex.en: They reserved some food for the winter.
ex.ko: 그들은 겨울을 위해 음식을 좀 비축해 두었다.

resident.en: a person who lives in a particular place

ex.en: Local residents complained about the noise.
The resident population of the island is small.

residential.en: used for or relating to houses rather than offices or shops

ex.en: They live in a quiet residential area.

resist.en: refuse to accept something or stop yourself from doing something you want to do

ex.en: She couldn't resist buying the shoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 신발을 사고 싶은 마음을 참을 수 없었다.

resolve.en: find a solution to a problem or disagreement

ex.en: They managed to resolve the conflict peacefully.

resolve.en: make a firm decision to do something

ex.en: She resolved to exercise every morning.

resort.cue: 다른 방법이 없어 어쩔 수 없이
resort.en: turn to a difficult or extreme action because there is no other choice

ex.en: They resorted to violence when talks failed.

resort.en: a place where people go on vacation

ex.en: We stayed at a beach resort last summer.

resource.en: a supply of something, such as money, materials, or energy, that can be used

ex.en: Water is a valuable natural resource.

respect.en: a feeling of admiration for someone because of their qualities or achievements

ex.en: She has great respect for her teacher.

respect.en: admire someone or treat them with consideration

ex.en: You should respect other people's opinions.

respectable.en: considered to be honest, good, and deserving respect

ex.en: He comes from a respectable family.

respond.en: say or do something as a reply or reaction

ex.en: He didn't respond to my email.

responsibility.en: a duty to deal with or take care of something

ex.en: Taking care of the dog is my responsibility.

responsible.en: having a duty to deal with or take care of something, or being the cause of something

ex.en: She is responsible for the whole project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트 전체에 대해 책임이 있다.

rest.en: a period of relaxing or sleeping

ex.en: You need some rest after the long trip.
ex.ko: 긴 여행 후에는 좀 쉬어야 한다.

rest.en: stop working or being active in order to relax

ex.en: He rested for an hour before dinner.
ex.ko: 그는 저녁 식사 전에 한 시간 쉬었다.

rest.en: the part of something that remains

ex.en: I'll finish the rest of the work tomorrow.

restore.en: bring something back to its original condition

ex.en: The museum restored the old painting.

restrict.en: limit the amount, size, or range of something

ex.en: The new law restricts the use of plastic bags.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 비닐봉지 사용을 제한한다.

retail.en: the selling of goods to customers in stores, not in large quantities to businesses

ex.en: He works in retail at a clothing store.

retain.en: continue to have or keep something

ex.en: The company managed to retain its best employees.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 최고의 직원들을 계속 붙잡아 두었다.

retell.en: tell a story again, sometimes in a different way

ex.en: She retold the story to her grandchildren.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손주들에게 그 이야기를 다시 들려주었다.

retrospect.en: a look back at something that happened in the past

ex.en: In retrospect, moving abroad was the right choice.
ex.ko: 돌이켜보면, 해외로 이주한 것은 옳은 선택이었다.

reunification.en: the process of bringing separated parts of a country back together

ex.en: They hoped for the reunification of the two countries.

reunify.en: bring separated parts of a country back together

ex.en: Germany was reunified in 1990.

revise.en: change something, such as a piece of writing or a plan, to improve or correct it

ex.en: She revised her essay before submitting it.

revise.en: (British English) study material again in preparation for an exam

ex.en: He is revising for his final exams.

revision.en: a change made to improve or correct something

ex.en: The contract needs a few revisions.
ex.ko: 그 계약서는 몇 가지 수정이 필요하다.

revision.en: (British English) the process of studying material again before an exam

ex.en: She spent the weekend on revision.
ex.ko: 그녀는 주말 동안 시험 복습을 했다.

reward.en: something given in return for good behavior or hard work

ex.en: Getting the job was a reward for her hard work.
ex.ko: 그 일자리를 얻은 것은 그녀의 노력에 대한 보상이었다.

reward.en: give something in return for good behavior or achievement

ex.en: The teacher rewarded the students with extra credit.

rewrite.en: write something again in a different or better way

ex.en: He had to rewrite the whole report.
ex.ko: 그는 보고서 전체를 다시 써야 했다.

rid.cue: 원치 않는 것을 치우다 (get rid of)
rid.en: make something free of an unwanted thing

ex.en: We need to get rid of these old boxes.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 낡은 상자들을 없애야 한다.

riddle.en: a puzzling question that has a clever or funny answer

ex.en: He asked me a tricky riddle.
That's a ridiculous excuse.

ripe.en: (of fruit) fully grown and ready to eat

ex.en: These bananas are perfectly ripe.

rise.en: increase in amount, number, or level

ex.en: Prices have risen sharply this year.

rise.en: an increase in amount or level

ex.en: There was a rise in the number of visitors.

risk.en: do something that might cause harm or loss

ex.en: He risked his life to save the child.
ex.ko: 그는 아이를 구하기 위해 목숨을 걸었다.

risk.en: the possibility that something bad might happen

ex.en: Smoking increases the risk of heart disease.
They stopped to rest at the roadside.

robot.en: a machine that can move and perform tasks automatically

ex.en: The factory uses robots to assemble cars.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 자동차를 조립하는 데 로봇을 사용한다.

rolling.en: moving on wheels, or (of land) rising and falling gently

ex.en: The road wound through rolling hills.
ex.ko: 그 길은 완만하게 이어지는 언덕들 사이로 구불구불 나 있었다.

rotten.en: decayed and no longer fit to eat or use

ex.en: The smell came from a rotten egg.
I've had a rotten day at work.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 직장에서 끔찍한 하루를 보냈다.

rough.en: having an uneven or bumpy surface, not smooth

ex.en: The rock had a rough surface.
ex.ko: 그 바위는 거친 표면을 가지고 있었다.

rough.en: not exact or precise, only approximate

ex.en: Can you give me a rough estimate of the cost?

routine.en: the usual set of things you do regularly, especially every day

ex.en: Exercise is part of her daily routine.

rubber.en: a strong elastic material used to make tires, boots, and other products

ex.en: The tires are made of rubber.

rubber.en: (British English) a small piece of rubber used to remove pencil marks

ex.en: Can I borrow your rubber?

rubbish.en: unwanted material that is thrown away

ex.en: Please put the rubbish in the bin.

rubbish.en: (informal) nonsense or something that is not true

ex.en: Don't talk rubbish, that's not what happened.
ex.ko: 허튼소리 하지 마, 그런 일 없었어.

rudely.en: in a way that is not polite

ex.en: He rudely interrupted her speech.

runaway.en: a person, especially a young one, who has left home or a difficult situation

ex.en: The shelter helps runaways find a safe place to stay.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 가출자들이 안전하게 머물 곳을 찾도록 돕는다.

runaway.en: out of control and impossible to stop

ex.en: The country is struggling with runaway inflation.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 걷잡을 수 없는 인플레이션으로 어려움을 겪고 있다.
